openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/ada.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing usagebased.Adapter for the usage-based charge lifecycle. Persists charges, realization runs, credit allocations, invoiced usage, payments, and detailed lines — all within context-propagated Ent transactions via entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every mutating method
patterns.description: Every public method that writes to DB wraps its body with entutils.TransactingRepo (returns value) or entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue (void). Never call a.db.X directly in a method body — always go through the tx-rebound client inside the closure.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (usagebased.ChargeBase, error) { return MapChargeBaseFromDB(tx.db.ChargeUsageBased.UpdateOneID(id).Save(ctx)) })

patterns.name: Validate inputs before opening a transaction
patterns.description: Every public method calls input.Validate() (or charge.Validate(), runID.Validate(), etc.) before the TransactingRepo call. Validation errors escape without starting a DB transaction.
patterns.example:
if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }
return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(...) { ... })

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad for ctx-propagated transactions
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx() (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self(). All three must stay in sync with every field on the adapter struct — if a new field is added to adapter, WithTx must copy it to the tx-rebound copy.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client(), logger: a.logger, metaAdapter: a.metaAdapter} }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions per file
patterns.description: Each file declares a blank var assertion for the sub-interface it implements (e.g. var _ usagebased.ChargeAdapter = (*adapter)(nil)). Add one per file when implementing a new sub-interface.

patterns.name: Config struct + Validate() + New() constructor
patterns.description: The adapter is always constructed via New(Config) which calls config.Validate() before returning. All dependencies live in Config. Never instantiate &adapter{} directly from outside the package.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (usagebased.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Mapper functions in mapper.go, not inline
patterns.description: All DB→domain conversions are delegated to named functions in mapper.go (MapChargeFromDB, MapChargeBaseFromDB, MapRealizationRunFromDB, MapRealizationRunBaseFromDB). Methods call these functions; they do not map inline.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt + namespace filter on all queries
patterns.description: Deletions set DeletedAt rather than hard-deleting rows; active-record queries must filter DeletedAtIsNil(). Every Ent query also scopes by Namespace to enforce multi-tenancy.

key_file_guides.role: Struct definition, Config/Validate/New constructor, and the Tx/WithTx/Self transaction plumbing — the core scaffolding every other file depends on.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field to the adapter struct without updating WithTx to copy it: the tx-rebound copy will silently lose the dependency and cause nil-pointer panics under concurrent charge advancement.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for ChargeUsageBased rows: CreateCharges, GetByID, GetByIDs, UpdateCharge, DeleteCharge.
key_file_guides.watch_for: expandRealizations builds the WithRuns eager-load chain; if a new run-level sub-entity is added to the schema, add its WithX() edge here and its OrErr() check + mapping in mapper.go.

key_file_guides.role: Pure DB→domain mapping functions with no Ent queries — the single source of mapping truth for this package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapRealizationRunFromDB uses OrErr() on every edge; if expandRealizations adds a new edge, this function must add the corresponding OrErr() check or it will silently return an error on every run expansion.

key_file_guides.role: UpsertRunDetailedLines (soft-delete existing lines then bulk-create with ON CONFLICT) and FetchDetailedLines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The ON CONFLICT clause lists specific columns; if the schema gains new upsertable fields, add the corresponding UpdateX() call in the Exec chain. FetchDetailedLines uses the DetailedLinesPresent flag as the authority — never infer presence from row count alone.

key_file_guides.role: CreateRealizationRun and UpdateRealizationRun for ChargeUsageBasedRuns rows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateRealizationRunInput uses mo.Option (IsPresent/OrEmpty) for partial updates — only set fields that are present to avoid overwriting with zero values; new fields must follow this pattern.

key_file_guides.role: CreateRunPayment and UpdateRunPayment for ChargeUsageBasedRunPayment rows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Explicit namespace mismatch check between runID.Namespace and payment.InvoicedCreate.Namespace is a hard error before TransactingRepo — preserve this guard for any new methods.

key_file_guides.role: CreateRunCreditRealization — bulk-creates credit allocation rows for a realization run using lo.Map + CreateBulk.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All rows share the runID's namespace; validate runID first and ensure new bulk builders inherit the same namespace.

key_file_guides.role: Integration test suite for UpsertRunDetailedLines upsert/soft-delete semantics, using testutils.InitPostgresDB and real Atlas migrations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests bootstrap metaadapter.New directly (not app/common) to avoid import cycles — follow this pattern for any new test suites added here.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.X directly inside a method body without TransactingRepo — bypasses the ctx-carried Ent transaction and causes partial writes under concurrent charge advancement.
Mapping DB rows inline inside query methods instead of delegating to named functions in mapper.go.
Hard-deleting rows — this package uses soft-delete (SetDeletedAt) everywhere for audit trail and idempotent realization runs.
Adding a field to the adapter struct without updating WithTx to propagate it to the tx-rebound copy.
Omitting namespace filter on any Ent query — breaks multi-tenancy isolation between customers.

decisions.decision: entutils.TransactingRepo wraps every write, even helpers that operate on a raw *entdb.Client
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; bypassing rebinding causes partial writes in the multi-step AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches flows (documented pitfall ctx-002).

decisions.decision: Soft-delete with DeletedAt + metaAdapter.DeleteRegisteredCharge instead of hard DELETE
decisions.rationale: Audit trail and idempotent realization reruns require historical charge state to remain queryable; hard deletes would break the realization run history.

decisions.decision: UpsertRunDetailedLines uses ON CONFLICT keyed on (namespace, chargeID, runID, ChildUniqueReferenceID) with DeletedAt IS NULL partial index
decisions.rationale: Rating reruns must replace existing detailed lines for the same logical line ID without creating duplicates, while selectively soft-deleting lines that no longer appear in the new set.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new mutation method following TransactingRepo discipline
